name: Surgical or Pathological Findings
description: Alternatively one of the following intraoperative findings from a surgeon or findings from a pathologist based on resections or an autopsy apply. Cases with surgical findings of intestinal perforation without evidence of primary necrosis or pneumatosis intestinalis (e.g., a spontaneous intestinal perforation) should not be reported as NEC.
dataElements: GlwA8737vfH JEZhgQZLvqW

name: Identified Organisms Recovered from the Surgical Site
description:
Organism(s) identified from the surgical site tissue by a culture or non-culture based microbiologic testing method which is performed for purposes of clinical diagnosis or treatment (for example, not Active Surveillance Culture/Testing (ASC/AST)).
For superficial incisional SSI this requires an aseptically obtained specimen from the superficial incision or subcutaneous tissue, for deep incisional SSI this requires a specimen from the deep soft tissues of the incision and for organ/space SSI this requires fluid or tissue from the organ/space.

name: Secondary Bloodstream Infection (BSI)/Sepsis
description: Secondary BSI that is thought to be seeded from the NEC or it's complications (e.g.; peritonitis after perforation). A BSI can be attributed to a NEC if it occurs in a 17-day period that includes the day of infection (=first NEC symptoms), 3 days prior, and 13 days after. Follow-up for secondary BSI is not mandatory.
